name: Museo Nacional de Antropología
address: Av. Paseo de la Reforma s/n, Bosque de Chapultepec, CDMX
description: One of the most important museums in the world, housing the largest collection of pre-Columbian artifacts in Mexico, including the famous Aztec Sun Stone.

name: Templo Mayor Museum
description: Built on the ruins of the main Aztec temple, this museum displays thousands of offerings and artifacts found during excavations right in the heart of the Historic Center.

name: National Art Museum (MUNAL)
description: Located in a stunning neoclassical building, MUNAL houses an impressive collection of Mexican art spanning from the 16th to the 20th century.

name: Memory and Tolerance Museum
address: Plaza Juárez s/n, Centro Histórico, CDMX
description: A powerful museum dedicated to promoting respect for human rights. It documents genocides throughout history and encourages visitors to reflect on tolerance and diversity.

name: Museo de la Ciudad de México
address: José María Pino Suárez 30, Centro Histórico, CDMX
description: Housed in a beautiful colonial palace, this museum tells the story of Mexico City from its pre-Hispanic origins to the present day through permanent and temporary exhibitions.

name: Jose Luis Cuevas Museum
description: A former convent turned contemporary art museum, showcasing works by José Luis Cuevas and other modern Mexican and international artists. Entry is free on Sundays.

name: National Museum of World Cultures
description: Located in the former Royal Mint building, this museum presents the cultural diversity of civilizations from around the world through archaeological and ethnographic collections.

name: Museo del Palacio de Bellas Artes
address: Av. Juárez s/n, Centro Histórico, CDMX
description: One of Mexico's most iconic cultural venues, featuring murals by Diego Rivera, Orozco, and Siqueiros, as well as rotating exhibitions of national and international art.

name: Interactive Museum of Economics (MIDE)
description: An interactive and hands-on museum that makes economics fun and accessible for all ages. Visitors can learn about money, trade, and financial systems through engaging exhibits.

name: Museo Franz Mayer
description: Housed in a 16th-century hospital, this museum displays an exquisite collection of decorative arts including furniture, ceramics, textiles, and silverwork from the colonial period.

name: Estanquillo Museum
address: Isabel La Católica 26, Centro Histórico, CDMX
description: A charming museum based on the personal collection of writer Carlos Monsiváis, featuring popular art, illustrations, photographs, and objects that reflect Mexican urban culture.

name: Museum of Caricature
description: Located in a stunning baroque building, this unique museum celebrates Mexican caricature and illustration art from the 19th century to the present, a fun visit for all ages.

name: Museo Kaluz
description: A science museum dedicated entirely to the phenomenon of light. Housed in a former colonial church, it features interactive experiments and installations exploring optics and physics.

name: Museo de la Secretaría de Hacienda y Crédito Público
description: Located in the old Archbishop's Palace, this museum houses an impressive art collection belonging to the Mexican government, featuring paintings, sculptures, and temporary exhibitions.

name: Former College of San Ildefonso
description: One of the most beautiful colonial buildings in Mexico City, birthplace of the Mexican muralist movement. It hosts rotating temporary exhibitions and preserves murals by Diego Rivera and Orozco.
